title: 5 free analyses experiment — run the positioning-pivot validation (per CLAUDE.md and docs/internal/product-positioning-2026-04-13.md). Goal: validate the '$100-500 musicologist you can't afford' frame by offering 5 free analyses to early users and measuring whether they convert / refer / produce shareable output. Out-of-scope: any landing-page copy in the old $29-99 frame (paused per 2026-04-14). Interactive-only — requires Ray's outreach judgment, real-user conversations, and positioning calls.
interactive_only_reason: Positioning + pricing validation with real early users. Needs Ray's outreach voice, judgment on who to approach, and live conversation follow-up. No bot-safe slice.

title: Reference-DB per-track fallback populates — when catalogdna's bot (launched via cdna-XXX trigger) can't find a clean playlist_url for a target album in bot_tasks.md populate work, take over the song-by-song workaround: (a) search YouTube for each track individually, (b) record per-track video IDs, (c) invoke the track-level downloader with those explicit URLs, (d) verify + commit. Judgment-heavy — the bot handles the common playlist-URL case; Ray handles the per-track fallback when the playlist path is flaky.
interactive_only_reason: Per-track URL search + manual sanity check on which videos match the canonical album sequence is judgment work. Bot handles the common case (cdna-004); Ray handles the fallback.

title: Dislikes-as-first-class-input design spec (per CLAUDE.md §'Product Direction: Dislikes as First-Class Input' and docs/internal/product-philosophy-dislikes-as-input.md). Not a current build target — this is the design-spec work that precedes build. Produce: (a) UI placement for the 'dislikes' input alongside existing influences field, (b) analytical model for how dislikes data feeds into the interpret layer, (c) how dislikes affect fingerprint narrative. Interactive-only — core product direction.
interactive_only_reason: Product-direction design spec. Taste work — which signal to surface, how to narrate, how it interacts with the honest-findings principle.

title: GS cycle trigger — permission for the dispatcher to launch catalogdna's existing autonomous bot. This task does NOT describe specific work; catalogdna's bot (run_bot.sh + CLAUDE-AUTONOMOUS.md) has its own native task queue in bot_tasks.md and picks from that. When GS's dispatcher picks this trigger, it runs `bash run_bot.sh ${cycle_budget_minutes}`, catalogdna's bot reads bot_tasks.md, does whatever's highest priority there (populate, test coverage, bug fix, audit, etc.), commits to bot/work, and exits. GS then runs its verification gate + reviewer on whatever diff landed. Ray maintains bot_tasks.md independently — that's where specific populate entries, test-coverage entries, etc. live. Re-add cdna-XXX triggers (cdna-005, cdna-006, ...) whenever Ray wants GS to kick off catalogdna's bot opportunistically. Empty bot_tasks.md → empty-diff cycle → verified_weak → task closes cleanly.

Reference-DB expansion — grow ref-db/artists/ from 171 entries to ~500-1000 canonical + commercial-safe curated set. Per CLAUDE.md §Quality Over Speed + §Honest Findings: DB should be objective/structural, not taste-filtered. Strategic shape (5 phases, see docs/sessions/2026-04-23-blitz-plan-v2.md cdna-006 plan): (1) pipeline audit — measure per-album wall-clock, verify basic-pitch on noisy audio, understand _quarantine/ policy, (2) curate target list — start Rolling Stone 500, supplement Acclaimed Music genre gaps, output CSV of ~500 candidates, (3) source acquisition — Free Music Archive + Jamendo + Bandcamp CC + curated yt-dlp (NOT Spotify, API dead for new apps Nov 2024; NOT Lakh/MetaMIDI, format mismatch — catalogdna pipeline starts from MP3 not raw MIDI), (4) bulk ingest via repeated cdna-004/005/... triggers against catalogdna's own bot_tasks.md populate entries — ~5-10 min/album × 500 = 40-80 wall-clock hrs overnight, ~$25-50 OpenRouter at Phase 7 qwen3.6-plus pricing, (5) quality regression spot-checks per CLAUDE.md §Pipeline Integrity. Ray-interactive time ~10-15 hrs across phases 1-2-5; bot-automated time the rest. Blocks on cdna-004 landing first (GS→catalogdna bot trigger). Rationale: commercial moat work — objective reference breadth is the diff between 'musicologist-you-cant-afford' and 'Ray's-self-analysis-plus-friends'.
interactive_only_reason: Phase 1 (pipeline audit) + Phase 2 (curation strategy + list build) + Phase 5 (QA regression) are all interactive. Phase 3 (acquisition scripting) and Phase 4 (bulk ingest) route through catalogdna's existing bot infrastructure once the strategic shape is set — at which point they become cdna-004-pattern triggers rather than novel interactive work.

title: PDF-export + Obsidian-wiki tier split — 2026-04-21 product-direction idea from Ray (captured during a walk). Current default output is the full Obsidian wiki. New direction: default output becomes an electronic PDF styled AS IF it were an Obsidian wiki (hyperlinked, branching, graph-feel visual language) — and the actual editable Obsidian wiki graduates to a higher-paid tier. Rationale: (a) more control over the PDF output — we own rendering, users don't edit around us; (b) a Claude Design pass can produce the PDF visual aesthetic cleanly (per CLAUDE.local.md §Skills-first tool integration — save a SKILL.md to catalogdna/.claude/skills/ once the aesthetic lands); (c) free/basic tier delivers real value without giving away the richer artifact; (d) monetization tier structure emerges naturally from format difference rather than feature-gate fiction. Existing tool options worth surveying: react-pdf skill at ~/.claude/skills/react-pdf/ (already proven via VC's manual generation); devforge has PDF tools per Ray mention; other OSS PDF-from-markdown generators possibly worth a look. Scope for this task (design spec, not build): (1) decide default-tier format (PDF-only vs PDF-+-basic-wiki-vs-something-else), (2) scope the Claude Design pass on the PDF aesthetic — pick a starter from awesome-claude-design that evokes the Obsidian-wiki feel (dark + typographic + graph-referential — Raycast, The Verge, or a custom brief are plausible vibes), (3) spec the tier gating (what content lives where, how the upgrade path works, pricing signal), (4) scope build effort against current catalogdna architecture + where render code lives. Does NOT commit to building — this is direction capture + spec. Interactive — product-tier direction is taste + business judgment.
interactive_only_reason: Product-tier direction + monetization design. Taste call on where the free/paid line draws; format decisions interact with current render architecture and cannot be automated away.

title: GS cycle trigger #2 — fires `bash run_bot.sh ${cycle_budget_minutes}` to run catalogdna's autonomous bot. Same shape as cdna-004; queued separately so a second back-to-back cycle can land in the same GS session. catalogdna's bot reads bot_tasks.md (refreshed 2026-04-24 evening for run 24, post-rule-relaxation) and picks the highest-priority remaining item. With the rule-relaxation in RULE-RELAXATION-2026-04-24-catalogdna-pipeline.md, the bot is now authorized to ship code in the analysis pipeline (`src/catalogdna/analyze/`, `convert/`, `discover/`, `reference/`, plus top-level analysis scripts). Run 24's queue starts with timbral extensions Phase 1 (P0) + hub-penalty distance fix (P1) — both genuinely move the analyzer needle per the 2026-04-13 phase-0 findings.

GS cycle trigger #3 — third trigger so a third back-to-back catalogdna cycle can run. catalogdna's bot picks whatever's still highest-priority in bot_tasks.md after cdna-004 + cdna-007 fire. Likely flow: cdna-004 picks P0 (timbral Phase 1), cdna-007 picks P1 (hub penalty), cdna-008 picks either P2 (timbral Phase 2 album aggregation) if P0 succeeded, OR Phase A/B rotation work if P0/P1 hit blockers. The bot's own run-judgment chooses; this trigger just authorizes a third cycle.

GS cycle trigger #4 — fourth trigger for tomorrow's session. Same pattern. Together with cdna-004/007/008 this gives the dispatcher 4 catalogdna runs in the queue; with `max_parallel_slots: 3` and ~13 registered projects, the picker will round-robin and catalogdna will get its share of slots. If 4 turns out to be too many for one session (other projects starve), Ray can flip the lowest-priority unused trigger to status: skipped at queue-trim time.
